title: podospermum radicosum
intro: Podospermum radicosum, also known as the poison thistle or green thistle, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its prickly leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Podospermum radicosum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. Podospermum radicosum is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
